---
title: "Bundle Calculus"
---

Bundle calculus starts with product-bundle projection and fiber vocabulary, then adds base-preserving transition sanity laws. Nontrivial atlases, overlaps, cocycles, connections, curvature, and holonomy remain future work.

::: {.learning-unit}

## What To Learn Here

The first bundle lesson is intentionally trivial: a base point and a fiber value are stored together, and projections recover each part. This gives the Living Book a safe vocabulary for base, fiber, and transition before any nontrivial bundle geometry is attempted.

:::

::: {.warning-box}
The current layer is product-bundle arithmetic and base-preserving transition sanity. Nontrivial atlas, overlap, cocycle, connection, curvature, and holonomy claims remain future work.
:::

## What To Notice

- A trivial bundle point has a base coordinate and a fiber coordinate.
- Projection theorems check that the two parts are recovered correctly.
- Transition theorems currently express base preservation and identity behavior only.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why should the project prove trivial bundle projection facts before adding nontrivial atlas or holonomy language?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0003"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0006"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0007"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0008"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0009"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_03_BUNDLE_CALCULUS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_03_BUNDLE_CALCULUS.md">Bundle Calculus</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
